name: French Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Add the first four ingredients to your bread machine according to the manufacturer's instructions.
2. Select the dough setting on the machine.
3. When the cycle is finished, remove the dough from the machine.
4. Let the dough rest for 10 minutes.
5. On a lightly floured surface, divide the dough in half and form each half into a 10x8-inch rectangle.
6. Roll up the rectangles jelly-roll style, starting from one of the long sides; seal well.
7. Pinch and pull the ends to create a taper.
8. Place the loaves seam side down on a greased baking sheet sprinkled with cornmeal.
9. Combine the egg white and water; brush some of it over the tops of the loaves.
10. Cover and let rise until nearly doubled (35-45 minutes).
11. With a sharp knife, make 3-4 diagonal cuts about 1/4 inch deep across the top of each loaf.
12. Bake in a 375°F oven for 20 minutes. Brush again with the remaining egg white and water mixture.
13. Continue baking for another 12-15 minutes or until the bread sounds hollow when tapped with your fingers.
14. Remove from the baking sheet and cool.
Makes 2 loaves
81 calories, 3g protein, 16g carbohydrates, 0g fat, 0mg cholesterol, 71mg sodium, 30mg potassium
Source: Bread Machine Bounty BH&G
From the recipe files of suzy@bestweb.net
